originalTitle: Sexual violence part of 'everyday life' in parts of Sudan, charity says
title: Kind Friends Are Helping People in Sudan Feel Safe!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hello, amazing friends! Your friendly news reporter from Africa is here! My name is [Reporter's Name - or simply 'Your African News Pal']. Today, we're going on a special journey to learn about a country called Sudan. It's a very big country in Africa, and it's full of wonderful people, just like you and me!

Where is Sudan?

Imagine a big map of Africa. Sudan is right there, in the north-eastern part! It's a land of sunshine and a very important river. This river is super long and it's called the River Nile. The River Nile helps grow yummy food and gives water to drink for many, many people. **Did you know?** The River Nile is the longest river in the whole world! It's like a giant blue ribbon winding across many countries, helping nature and people along its way!

What's Happening in Sudan?

Sometimes, in some parts of Sudan, things are not very peaceful or happy. This means that some people, especially grown-ups, find it hard to do their everyday things. What are everyday things? Like going to get water from the well, or finding food to eat for their families, or just walking outside to see their friends and neighbours. Imagine if you couldn't go outside to play your favourite game, or if your mum or dad felt too scared to go to the shop to buy milk. That's how some people in Sudan feel sometimes. They wish they could just live their lives happily and safely, just like everyone else in the world deserves.

Why Do People Feel Unsafe?

Sadly, there are sometimes bad things happening in these areas. It's not because of the good people in Sudan themselves, but sometimes grown-ups have big fights. These fights make it hard for other people to be safe. It makes people feel very scared to do simple things that we all do every day without thinking twice. When people feel scared, they can't always get the clean water they need. They might not get enough food to eat. Sometimes, children can't even go to school because it's not safe. This makes their lives very, very tough. No one should ever feel scared to do these simple, important things to live a happy life. **Did you know?** Children in Sudan love to play games and learn, just like you! They play with handmade toys and enjoy telling stories with their friends and family.

Who is Helping Our Friends?

But guess what? There are many kind and brave people who want to help! These helpers often work for special groups called 'charities'. A 'charity' is like a big team of super-helpers. They collect money and help from other kind people all around the world. Then, they use that help to make things better for those who are having a tough time. These charities are working super hard in Sudan. They try to make sure people can get clean water. They try to give yummy food to families who are hungry. And most importantly, they try to help everyone feel safe, listen to their worries, and make them feel cared for. They are like a big, warm hug for those who need it most.

Helping Hands Make a Big Difference

Imagine a hand holding another hand. That's what charities do! They reach out their kind hands to help people who are having a really tough time. They want everyone in Sudan, especially children, to feel safe, happy, and free to play and learn and dream big dreams. They also talk to the grown-ups who are fighting and ask them to stop. They try to find ways for everyone to live together peacefully, like good neighbours. It's a big, big job, but these helpers are very determined and have big, kind hearts. They never give up trying to make things better. **Did you know?** Many people in Africa love to dance and sing! It's a wonderful way to tell stories, share feelings, and celebrate life and community.

Everyday Life Should Be Safe for Everyone

Think about your day. You wake up, eat breakfast, maybe go to school, play with your friends, help at home, and then sleep. All these are everyday activities. It's so, so important that everyone, everywhere in the world, can do these things without any worry or fear. Everyone deserves to feel safe in their own home and community. In Sudan, some people are saying that doing these everyday things feels scary. That's why the charities are shouting loudly and saying, "Hey! Everyone deserves to be safe!" They want to remind everyone that feeling safe is a very important right for all people, big and small.

What Can We Learn from This News?

From this news, we learn that it's super important to be kind to everyone, no matter where they live. We also learn that when bad things happen, there are always good people who step up to help. It shows us the amazing power of caring, sharing, and trying our best to make the world a better place. Even though things are tough in some places, the helpers are there, bringing hope and trying their hardest to make sure every child can smile and play without any fear. They are truly everyday heroes! **Did you know?** Africa is a continent with many different amazing animals, like lions, elephants, giraffes, and zebras! It's full of incredible nature and beautiful landscapes.

Wishing for Peace for All

Our biggest wish for Sudan and all its amazing people is that they can soon live every day feeling safe, happy, and free. We hope that all the grown-ups will learn to share and be kind, so that children can grow up strong, joyful, and full of hope. Let's send our kindest thoughts and best wishes to our friends in Sudan! Thank you for listening, super friends!
summary: Kind charities are helping people in Sudan feel safe and happy again so they can do everyday things like getting water, food, and going to school without being scared.
whyMatters: It's important because everyone, everywhere in the world, deserves to feel safe and happy doing their everyday activities, and kind people are working hard to make that happen for our friends in Sudan.

title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad

title: Failed experiment as England get grim glimpse of life without Kane
content: England's World Cup hopes rest on captain Harry Kane after a grim taste of life without him in the defeat against Japan at Wembley, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

title: 'Scotland fans can fret - but they need to keep perspective too'

title: Angry Wales boss Bellamy shows his new old self
content: Craig Bellamy showed his fiery temper for the first time as Wales boss in their Northern Ireland draw - a rage that was evident during his playing days.
